acceptance_criteria: Agent Chat page is the default landing page and shows all active agent sessions with live-streaming output (OutputChunk, Thinking events) in a chat-style interface.
Users can send messages/instructions to running agents via a chat input, with the message delivered through the agent-runner IPC channel.
Each agent session shows: task context, current phase, confidence/verdict from PhaseDecision, elapsed time, and token usage.
Agent output renders structured content: code blocks with syntax highlighting, JSON decisions with collapsible detail, file diffs inline, and thinking/reasoning in a distinct visual style.
Chat history persists across page navigations and reconnects via SSE replay (last-event-id).
Task Board page shows all tasks in a kanban-style board grouped by status columns (backlog, ready, in-progress, blocked, done) with drag-and-drop status transitions.
Workflow Monitor page shows active workflows as a phase pipeline visualization with real-time progress, decision gates, and drill-down to agent output per phase.
Planning Hub page consolidates vision, requirements, and task creation into a guided flow with AI-assisted refinement.
Navigation prominently features agent count badge and notification indicators for agent events (decisions, errors, handoff requests).
All pages are responsive and functional on tablet/mobile viewports with touch-friendly controls.
Page structure supports deep-linking: /agents/:runId opens a specific agent chat session directly.
Dashboard provides at-a-glance metrics: active agents, queue depth, recent decisions, throughput (tasks/hour), and error rate.
description:
Redesign the AO web application with agent interaction as the primary experience. The current web app is a CRUD management interface for tasks, requirements, and workflows. The new design makes **agent chat the centerpiece** — users should spend most of their time watching agents work, steering their decisions, and reviewing output, not clicking through admin forms.

## Design Philosophy

The web app should feel like a **mission control for AI agents**, not a project management tool. The primary loop is:
1. See what agents are doing right now (Agent Chat)
2. Review their decisions and output (Workflow Monitor)
3. Queue up more work (Task Board)
4. Plan what needs to happen (Planning Hub)

## Page Structure

### 1. Agent Chat (Primary — `/agents`)
The default landing page. Split into:
- **Session List** (left panel): All active agent runs, grouped by task. Shows agent model, phase, elapsed time, and status indicator (thinking, writing, idle, error).
- **Chat View** (main panel): Selected agent's live output stream in a conversational layout:
  - Agent thinking/reasoning shown in collapsible blocks with distinct styling
  - Code changes shown as inline diffs with syntax highlighting
  - JSON decisions (PhaseDecision, research_required, implementation_result) rendered as structured cards
  - File modifications shown with before/after previews
  - Error output highlighted with stack trace formatting
- **Context Sidebar** (right panel, collapsible): Task details, linked requirements, phase pipeline progress, decision history for current workflow.
- **Chat Input** (bottom): Send instructions/guidance to the running agent. Support for: plain text, code snippets, file references.
- **Session URL**: `/agents/:runId` deep-links to a specific session.

### 2. Task Board (`/tasks`)
Kanban-style board replacing the current list view:
- Columns: Backlog | Ready | In Progress | Blocked | Done
- Cards show: title, priority badge, assignee, linked workflow status indicator
- Drag-and-drop between columns triggers status transition API calls
- Click card → slide-out detail panel (not full page navigation)
- Quick actions: assign, set priority, start workflow, add dependency
- Filter bar: by priority, type, assignee, tag, requirement
- "New Task" button opens inline creation form

### 3. Workflow Monitor (`/workflows`)
Real-time workflow execution dashboard:
- Active workflows shown as horizontal phase pipelines (requirements → research → implementation → code-review → testing)
- Each phase node shows: status (pending/running/passed/failed/rework), duration, agent model, decision verdict
- Click a phase → opens agent chat for that phase's run
- Decision gate visualization: confidence meter, risk indicator, evidence list, verdict (advance/rework/fail)
- Rework loops shown as cycle indicators on the pipeline
- Completed workflows shown in a timeline below

### 4. Planning Hub (`/planning`)
Consolidated planning interface:
- **Vision** tab: Vision document editor with AI refinement
- **Requirements** tab: Requirement list with status pipeline, priority matrix, acceptance criteria editor
- **Generate** tab: AI-assisted task generation from requirements — select requirements, generate task breakdown, review and approve
- Links between requirements → tasks → workflows visible as breadcrumb trails

### 5. Dashboard (`/dashboard`)
At-a-glance operational metrics:
- Active agents count with model breakdown
- Queue depth (ready tasks waiting for agents)
- Recent decisions (last 10) with verdict/confidence sparkline
- Throughput: tasks completed per hour/day
- Error rate and recent failures
- Daemon health status with uptime
- Quick actions: start/stop daemon, pause scheduling

### 6. Settings (`/settings`)
- Daemon configuration (max agents, interval, timeouts)
- Agent runtime config editor (model selection, phase directives)
- Storage backend selection (json-file, sqlite, postgres)
- Notification preferences

## Technical Constraints
- Must use existing Axum web server and embedded static assets pattern
- Must consume existing /api/v1 REST endpoints (extend as needed)
- SSE for real-time updates (existing /api/v1/events endpoint)
- WebSocket for bidirectional agent chat (new endpoint needed)
- React + TypeScript frontend with Vite build
- No additional frontend framework dependencies beyond what exists
title: Agent-first web application redesign with chat-centric page structure
